senses.definition: a type of advertising sign consisting of two boards, worn one on the front and one on the back of a person, often used to promote a business or event

examples.x: A sandwich board outside the cafe caught my eye with a special offer for coffee.
The movie theater used a sandwich board to show the times for the latest screenings.
She carried a sandwich board around the fair to promote her handmade jewelry.
The sandwich board outside the bookstore had a quote from a famous author.

senses.definition: a type of portable advertising sign, typically made of wood or metal, that is hinged at the top and can be folded to resemble the letter "A," and often used to promote a business or event

examples.x: The café placed an A-board outside to advertise their daily specials.
The event organizers set up an A-board to direct people to the registration desk.
You can find the store's opening hours on the A-board near the entrance.
They used an A-board to announce the new product launch in front of their shop.
The A-board was covered in colorful signs promoting the weekend sale.

senses.definition: a small, often sexually suggestive advertisement card, typically found in telephone boxes and public toilets in some cities, used to promote the services of prostitutes or escort agencies

examples.x: I found a tart card on the sidewalk near the club last night.
He picked up a tart card that was left on the bench while walking home.
The police removed several tart cards from the street corners during the sweep.
The area near the nightclub was littered with tart cards, making the place seem unwelcoming.

senses.definition: a person who carries or wears advertising materials, often in the form of a sign or costume, to promote a business or product

examples.x: The man walked around the city as a human billboard, wearing a shirt that advertised a new restaurant.
The idea of being a human billboard might sound strange, but some people see it as a fun way to make money.
As a human billboard, she agreed to wear branded clothing for a year in exchange for a monthly payment.
Some companies use human billboards to grab attention in crowded places like sports events or concerts.

senses.definition: any kind of visual graphic display intended to convey information to an audience, typically used for advertising, wayfinding, or identification purposes
a long piece of cloth with a design or message, which is hung in public places, typically used to represent something at events
a small piece of paper that has information about something being advertised, usually printed in color and handed out to people by hand
a large printed picture or notice, typically used for advertising or decoration
a type of advertisement printed on a large piece of paper
a type of outdoor advertising material that is affixed to a sidewalk or other paved surface to convey a message or promote a product or servic

examples.x: The store used a sidewalk decal to advertise their grand opening.
The sidewalk decal was so bright that it caught everyone's attention.
I noticed a sidewalk decal directing people to the nearby art gallery.
The sidewalk decal reminded pedestrians of the upcoming street fair.

senses.definition: a type of non-rigid airship characterized by its elongated shape, buoyancy provided by gas-filled chambers, and the absence of a rigid internal framework
a large banner or sign displayed on a street pole or lamppost for outdoor advertising or event promotion
a large electronic display used for presenting video content, advertisements, or visual information in public spaces or venues

examples.x: The video board at the stadium displayed highlights from the game during halftime.
The concert featured a massive video board that showed close-up shots of the performers.
The mall installed a new video board in the entrance to advertise upcoming sales.
The company ran a commercial on the video board downtown to attract more customers.
The video board in the sports arena showed the score and team stats throughout the match.

senses.definition: a type of outdoor advertising that involves displaying advertisements or messages on a moving vehicle

examples.x: The company used a mobile billboard to advertise their new product across the city.
The local restaurant decided to use a mobile billboard to advertise their special deals during lunch hours.
We saw a mobile billboard driving around downtown with an ad for a local concert.
They rented a mobile billboard to promote their grand opening event in different neighborhoods.

senses.definition: a form of advertising that involves covering vehicles with vinyl graphics or decals featuring promotional messages, logos, or designs

examples.x: The company decided to use wrap advertising on their delivery trucks to promote their new product.
Wrap advertising is a great way to turn your car into a moving billboard and reach people everywhere you go.
I noticed a lot of wrap advertising while driving downtown, especially on taxis and buses.
Wrap advertising can be a cost-effective solution for small businesses to get noticed in busy areas.

senses.definition: advertisements or promotional messages placed on various modes of public transportation, such as buses, trains, subway stations, or trams

examples.x: The company decided to use transit advertising by placing ads on buses and trains around the city.
You can often spot eye-catching transit advertising while traveling through the downtown area.
The city is covered with transit advertising, from bus wraps to train station posters.
The brand used transit advertising to promote their latest products on subway cars during rush hour.

senses.definition: a fabric or vinyl banner displayed on a pole or mast to promote events, businesses, or organizations in outdoor spaces

examples.x: The town square was filled with flags advertising the upcoming festival.
The business hung a flag outside its store to attract customers.
Colorful flags lined the streets, promoting the local fair.
The event organizers placed flags around the park to guide attendees.

senses.definition: a large outdoor advertising display that covers an entire wall or building façade with eye-catching graphics or messages

examples.x: The new wallscape on the side of the building caught everyone’s attention during rush hour.
We drove past a huge wallscape promoting the latest movie; it was impossible to miss.
The company decided to invest in a wallscape to advertise their upcoming event in the city center.
Wallscapes are becoming a popular form of outdoor advertising in major cities.
The marketing team designed a vibrant wallscape to attract tourists to the new museum.

senses.definition: a promotional or advertising display located near the checkout counter or point of purchase in a retail store or establishment

examples.x: The store set up a point-of-sale display near the counter to promote their latest snacks.
At the point-of-sale display, I saw a buy-one-get-one-free deal on coffee mugs.
The store’s point-of-sale display had a small rack of chocolates right next to the register.
They added a point-of-sale display showcasing new phone accessories right by the checkout.

senses.definition: the use of large inflatable structures, such as balloons, inflatable characters, or custom shapes, for promotional or advertising purposes

examples.x: Inflatable advertising is a fun way to get people talking about your brand.
The event organizers set up inflatable advertising around the venue to advertise their sponsors.
The company used inflatable advertising to promote their new product at the festival.
The inflatable advertising display at the car dealership was hard to miss as we drove by.

senses.definition: a form of advertising or message display that involves using a small airplane to write words or draw images in the sky using smoke
